Name: dynamic_precision_allocation
Title: Dynamic Precision Allocation for Efficient Latency-Bounded Inference
Short Hypothesis: Dynamic allocation of numerical precision based on real-time activation statistics can maintain accuracy while minimizing latency in low-precision inference, surpassing static allocation strategies.
Related Work: Previous works such as 'Spectral Quantization Routing' and 'Anda' focus on static profiling or post-training adjustments for quantization. My proposal extends this by introducing a real-time, adaptive mechanism that utilizes activation geometry to allocate precision dynamically, thus addressing the limitations of static strategies and enhancing the responsiveness to input variations.
Abstract: In the context of latency-critical machine learning inference, maintaining accuracy while minimizing computational overhead is paramount. Existing methods often rely on static precision allocation, which can lead to inefficiencies and inaccuracies when faced with dynamic input distributions. We propose a novel framework for Dynamic Precision Allocation (DPA) that leverages real-time activation statistics to adaptively allocate numerical precision during inference. By utilizing a PAC-Bayes grounded mechanism to compute activation geometry, we identify which components can tolerate low precision and which require higher fidelity. Our method dynamically adjusts precision on-the-fly, ensuring rigorous adherence to latency budgets while maintaining or even enhancing overall model performance. We will validate DPA through rigorous empirical evaluations across various benchmarks, demonstrating its capability to achieve superior throughput-accuracy Pareto frontiers compared to existing static and heuristic approaches. This research not only provides a pathway towards more efficient inference in resource-constrained environments but also contributes to the theoretical understanding of adaptive resource allocation in machine learning.
Experiments: Evaluate DPA against static quantization strategies using a set of benchmark models across different tasks.
Measure latency and accuracy trade-offs, specifically focusing on throughput-accuracy metrics.
Implement a baseline static precision allocation for comparison, analyzing the performance decay in dynamically varying input scenarios.
Conduct ablation studies to isolate the impact of activation geometry on precision allocation decisions.
Risk Factors and Limitations: Potential risks include the difficulty in achieving real-time computation of activation metrics without introducing unacceptable latency. Additionally, the framework's performance may vary significantly across different model architectures, necessitating careful tuning.
